why: Opened per flag: OVERDONE conf 3 — Gemini 3.5 Flash: Google bets next AI wave on agents, not chatbots
Opened per flag: OVERDONE conf 3 — Google pitches AI agent ecosystem to consumers at I/O

5 days held; -2.91% unrealized loss and stock trading below cost basis despite the Google I/O catalyst being old news by now. The thesis (cloud-based SaaS untouched by AI agents) is mechanically sound, but the sympathy fade is not materializing as expected — price has not recovered. Trim 50% to cut tail risk and lock dry powder; reassess BLKB's thesis strength vs. broader SaaS momentum before day 10.

Opened per flag: OVERDONE conf 4 — Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026 with AI agents replacing blue links

NCNO was flagged on day 0, already down 5.02% intraday. The current flag re-surfaces NCNO with identical Google Search trigger and notes threat_assessment='minimal' (not 'none'), and the filings evidence shows generic AI/ML competitive risk language rather than crisp evidence of no search-agent pathway into regulated banking workflows. The flag itself acknowledges 'sympathy drop is thematic SaaS-is-doomed reflex' — which means the panic may not be unjustified, just premature. More critically: day-0 entry + immediate 5% drawdown on a short-window thesis signals poor entry timing. Exit to preserve capital and reduce sector concentration (currently 4 tech positions, 3 of which are day-0 or underwater).

VRRM is in catastrophic drawdown: -71.82% after day 0 on the Google Search I/O trigger. The current flag re-surfaces VRRM with threat_assessment='none' and strong filings evidence (municipal contracts, camera hardware, court-admissible evidentiary chains have zero search-overhaul exposure). However, the 10-Q already flags a critical real risk: 'loss of one of the three large Commercial Services customers currently in renewal.' This is NOT sympathy-fade territory; this is idiosyncratic deterioration on a real business risk. The -71% move suggests the market has priced in customer loss or regulatory bans (Ontario speed-enforcement ban is real). Holding a position this destroyed on a 5-15 day clock violates the discipline — the sympathy thesis is drowned out by fundamental risk. Exit immediately.

Days held = 8; if catalyst is company-specific (earnings, guidance, customer loss), sympathy thesis is invalidated. EXIT per Screen 1 discipline: broken thesis overrides holding window.

5 days held, -13.63% unrealized, move_pct -14.5. LRN is a regulated K-12 virtual school operator (per-pupil state funding model); filings evidence in new flag confirms thesis that AI tutoring sympathy is mechanically wrong. However, the stock has fallen sharply without recovery — sympathy may be morphing into genuine uncertainty on enrollment trends or state funding. Trim 50% to lock in some cash, reassess thesis integrity on next data release. If filings show actual enrollment headwinds, exit remainder.

Days held: 6 of 15. Unrealized P&L: -13.81% (largest loss in portfolio). Original Apple Siri/Image Playground catalyst. Today's flag (Meta AI Mode) is a SECOND AI-event trigger hitting the same name within 1 trading day—cumulative sympathy pile-on, not isolated. Volume spike (5.35x) on the -14.5% move signals genuine liquidation, not retail panic. Stride's core thesis (regulated K-12 virtual school funding model) is intact, but the dual-trigger acceleration suggests market repricing edtech exposure across the basket. TRIM 50% position (sell ~1.3 shares, ~50% of position) to lock in a half-size hold through day 15 and preserve capital for higher-conviction opportunities.

7 days held, down 14.39%, confidence 4 flag from Apple Siri overhaul (unrelated to Stride's per-pupil state-funded school model). New flag from Meta AI Mode catalyst shows confidence 3, threat='minimal', panic='unjustified' — reinforces the thesis that Stride's regulated K-12 accredited diploma business is orthogonal to consumer AI tutoring apps. However, the -14.39% move is severe and the 5.35x volume spike on the Meta flag suggests real forced selling or broader charter-funding fear unrelated to this trigger. Trim 40% position size (0.5 of 1.3069 shares) to lock in tax loss and de-risk. Thesis not broken, but weakness is exceeding sympathy-fade parameters.

days_held = 14, at the upper boundary of the 5-15 day holding window. P&L is -6.22% and the sympathy-fade thesis (Anthropic Claude Fable 5 irrelevant to Ambarella's video codec/compression business) remains intact on filings review, but holding-window discipline mandates exit at day 14 with no fresh recovery catalyst. The position has not recovered; Screen 1 is built to exit after 15 days regardless of P&L if sympathy fade hasn't materialized.

days_held = 14, at holding-window boundary. P&L is +0.34%, virtually flat; thesis (cybersecurity researcher pushback on Fable guardrails doesn't impair Tenable's vulnerability-management business) is intact, but the 15-day holding window is the hard cutoff for Screen 1's sympathy-fade discipline. Position is neither recovering sharply nor showing conviction that the fade is sustained; exit per protocol.

days_held = 12, but P&L is +7.97% (thesis has played out successfully). Today's flag re-scores PLAB under Claude Tag catalyst with identical threat_assessment='none' and confidence=4, but the sympathy fade has already reversed — position is up 8%. Holding to day 15 adds diminishing conviction-building value; lock in the sympathy-fade win and redeploy capital to fresh opportunities with earlier entry points and cleaner setups.

days_held = 12, but P&L is -14.2% (severe underperformance vs. the sympathy-fade thesis mandate). Catalyst was Apple's AI Siri overhaul (unrelated to Stride's k-12 online education delivery); filings show no structural AI threat. The 14% drawdown is well beyond sympathy-move noise and suggests either (1) a separate negative catalyst on LRN we are not tracking, or (2) the thesis was misclassified. Exit to stop the bleed and free capital.

days_held = 15, holding window max reached. Thesis (Slack AI teammate is sympathy, not real threat to factory-floor vision) may remain intact, but Screen 1 discipline mandates exit after 15 trading days regardless of P&L. Current unrealized +10.38% is a win; exit and redeploy cash.

Given that the primary ONTO position (days_held=2) is already held and the thesis is intact across both, this duplicate entry violates position discipline — exit the day-0 tranche and consolidate into the 2-day position.

First DOCN tranche: 10 days held, down 8.92%. OpenAI custom chip catalyst is thinner than Anthropic/Google agent catalysts (less direct sympathy spillover to a cloud infrastructure provider). Holding two tranches totaling 8.0 DOCN shares at cumulative 10.89% loss suggests position is overcrowded. Trim 2 shares from this tranche to raise cash and reduce sector concentration.

Second DOCN tranche: 5 days held, down 12.85%. Holding two separate DOCN entries (likely from distinct flag dates/catalysts) creates redundancy and sector drift. Trim 1.5 shares from this position to de-risk and align portfolio. Remaining 2.4 shares preserves exposure to either thesis that recovers.

Holding window maximum reached (days_held=15). Screen 1 discipline: sympathy-fade thesis has a fixed 5-15 day window; at 15 days with no recovery (-15.62% unrealized), the fade trade is exhausted. New flag on AEIS today (Claude Cowork, threat_assessment=none, filings-positive) is a separate buy signal, not a reason to hold the old position. Exit to free capital.

Holding window maximum reached (days_held=15). Despite strong recovery (+18.28% unrealized), the Screen 1 thesis window has expired. Thesis still intact (no fresh negative news), but discipline requires exit at day 15 to prevent drift into long-term hold. Lock in gains and redeploy.

Holding window maximum reached (days_held=14, approaching boundary at 15). Thesis remains intact but new flag on PLAB today (Claude Cowork, threat_assessment=none, filings-positive for photomasks) is mechanically stronger than the original flag (Anthropic Claude Tag, unrelated). Exit original position and rebuy via new flag if conviction/exploratory tier warrants.

11 days held, -8.57% unrealized; Meta's Pocket gaming app is a low-credibility catalyst (vibe-coded, niche consumer gaming has zero bearing on semiconductor process control). Thesis remains intact but position underwater after 11 days suggests market inefficiency is not healing. Trim half position to lock liquidity, let remainder ride to day-15 if thesis holds.

1 day held, -7.58% unrealized, five-day drop -14.88%. Original flag (from yesterday's run) cited 'partial' panic calibration due to potential earnings whisper. Today's received flag reiterates that risk, noting hyperscaler price competition and SMB churn risk. The deep 5-day drawdown suggests real business concern beyond sympathy fade. Trim 50% position to lock in downside while retaining exposure in case sympathy fade does recover; exit the remainder if thesis breaks further.

1 day held, +2.74% unrealized, five-day change -2.73%. Today's flag (received this run) acknowledges DUOL's genuine AI exposure: filings explicitly name free ChatGPT competitors and improving translation as real threats. Confidence is only 3 (vs. 4 for other Siri-basket names), and threat_assessment is 'indirect' not 'none'. Unlike accredited K-12 (LRN) or vertical SaaS (TYL/BLKB/VRRM), language learning IS materially AI-exposed. The gamified habit loop is a moat, but it's thinner than the court-records/ERP moat. Trim 50% to reduce conviction; retain 50% as a test.

days_held = 15, at the Screen 1 holding window max. Thesis was unjustified sympathy on Anthropic Claude announcement; five_day_change_pct is +0.89 (slight recovery), but window discipline requires exit. Prevent drift into 'wait for recovery' mindset on a trade built for 5-15 days.

days_held = 2, but five_day_change_pct = -14.88 (deep move) and threat_assessment = 'indirect' with 'partial' panic_calibration. Filings show real AI/ML competitive pressure and hyperscaler risk, not pure sympathy. The move may contain genuine negative sentiment beyond the GPT-5.6 headline. Trim 50% to lock in downside protection; hold remainder through window in case it's still sympathy fade, but reduce exposure given the deeper drawdown and higher threat profile vs. peers.

days_held = 2, confidence = 3, threat_assessment = 'indirect' with 'partial' panic_calibration. Unlike accredited K-12 or government SaaS, consumer language learning faces genuine AI-native competition (free ChatGPT, translation APIs). Filings explicitly flag this. The -2.73% five-day move is modest, suggesting market is pricing real risk, not just sympathy. Trim 50% to de-risk; hold remainder through window but at reduced size given structural AI exposure.
